Aortic Dissection.who_gets_it: Older men with HTN; younger patients with Marfan/Ehlers-Danlos.
Aortic Dissection.risk_factors: Chronic HTN, Bicuspid aortic valve, Cocaine use.
Aortic Dissection.typical_presentation: Sudden onset 'tearing' chest pain radiating to the back.
Aortic Dissection.moa: Intimal tear creates a false lumen in the aortic wall.
Aortic Dissection.step1_buzzwords: Tearing/Ripping pain, BP discrepancy between arms, Mediastinal widening.
Aortic Dissection.lab_buzzwords: Markedly elevated D-Dimer (rules out if negative).

Appendicitis.typical_presentation: Periumbilical pain migrating to RLQ, fever, anorexia.
Appendicitis.moa: Obstruction of appendiceal lumen leads to bacterial overgrowth.
Appendicitis.step1_buzzwords: McBurney's point, Rovsing sign, Psoas sign, Obturator sign.

Acute Angle-Closure Glaucoma.risk_factors: Mydriatics (pupil dilation), dark rooms, Sulfa drugs.
Acute Angle-Closure Glaucoma.typical_presentation: Sudden eye pain, blurry vision, 'halos' around lights, nausea.
Acute Angle-Closure Glaucoma.moa: Iris blocks trabecular meshwork; rapid intraocular pressure rise.
Acute Angle-Closure Glaucoma.step1_buzzwords: Steamy cornea, Mid-dilated fixed pupil, Rock-hard eye.

Guillain-Barre Syndrome (GBS).who_gets_it: Any age, usually 1-2 weeks after an infection.
Guillain-Barre Syndrome (GBS).typical_presentation: Symmetric ascending muscle weakness and loss of reflexes.
Guillain-Barre Syndrome (GBS).moa: Autoimmune demyelination of peripheral nerves via molecular mimicry.
Guillain-Barre Syndrome (GBS).lab_buzzwords: Albuminocytologic dissociation (High protein/Normal WBC in CSF).

Acute Cholecystitis.who_gets_it: The 4 F's: Female, Fat, Fertile, Forty.
Acute Cholecystitis.typical_presentation: Constant RUQ pain, right shoulder radiation, fever, vomiting.
Acute Cholecystitis.moa: Gallstone obstruction of the cystic duct causing inflammation.

Hypertrophic Cardiomyopathy (HCM).risk_factors: Autosomal Dominant; beta-myosin heavy chain mutations.
Hypertrophic Cardiomyopathy (HCM).typical_presentation: Dyspnea on exertion, syncope, sudden death in athletes.
Hypertrophic Cardiomyopathy (HCM).moa: Asymmetric septal hypertrophy causing LVOT obstruction.
Hypertrophic Cardiomyopathy (HCM).step1_buzzwords: Systolic Anterior Motion (SAM), S4, Murmur softer with squatting.

Pulmonary Hypertension.risk_factors: BMPR2 mutation, Connective tissue disease, Left heart failure.
Pulmonary Hypertension.typical_presentation: Progressive exertional dyspnea, Raynaud's, precordial heave.
Pulmonary Hypertension.moa: Proliferation of pulmonary artery smooth muscle; plexiform lesions.
Pulmonary Hypertension.step1_buzzwords: Loud S2, Plexiform lesions, Right ventricular heave.

Hyperparathyroidism (Primary).who_gets_it: Postmenopausal women; MEN 1 and MEN 2A families.
Hyperparathyroidism (Primary).moa: Overproduction of PTH leading to excessive bone resorption.
Hyperparathyroidism (Primary).step1_buzzwords: Osteitis fibrosa cystica, Salt and pepper skull, Subperiosteal resorption.
Hyperparathyroidism (Primary).lab_buzzwords: High PTH, High Calcium, Low Phosphorus.

Squamous Cell Lung Carcinoma.typical_presentation: Cough, hemoptysis, hilar mass, right arm edema (SVC syndrome).
Squamous Cell Lung Carcinoma.moa: Malignant transformation of bronchial squamous metaplasia.
Squamous Cell Lung Carcinoma.step1_buzzwords: Hilar mass, Keratin pearls, Intercellular bridges.

Acute Intermittent Porphyria.risk_factors: Alcohol, Sulfa drugs, starvation, Cytochrome P450 inducers.
Acute Intermittent Porphyria.typical_presentation: Abdominal pain (no tenderness), confusion, dark urine.
Acute Intermittent Porphyria.step1_buzzwords: 5 P's: Painful abdomen, Port-wine urine, Polyneuropathy, Psychological distress, Precipitated by drugs.

Prostate Cancer.risk_factors: Age, African American race, High-fat diet.
Prostate Cancer.typical_presentation: Often asymptomatic; back pain and incontinence (if metastatic).
Prostate Cancer.moa: Adenocarcinoma of the posterior lobe (peripheral zone).
Prostate Cancer.step1_buzzwords: Peripheral zone mass, Blastic bone lesions, Increased ALP.

Rocky Mountain Spotted Fever.who_gets_it: Hikers/Campers in NC, TN, OK, AR.
Rocky Mountain Spotted Fever.typical_presentation: High fever, headache, rash on wrists/palms moving inward.
Rocky Mountain Spotted Fever.moa: Rickettsia rickettsii obligate intracellular infection of endothelial cells.
Rocky Mountain Spotted Fever.step1_buzzwords: Centripetal rash (outer to inner), Palms and Soles rash.

Acute Myeloid Leukemia (AML).risk_factors: Prior radiation, Alkylating chemo, Down Syndrome.
Acute Myeloid Leukemia (AML).typical_presentation: Fatigue, easy bruising, bleeding gums, bone pain.
Acute Myeloid Leukemia (AML).step1_buzzwords: Auer rods, MPO positive, Gingival hyperplasia (M5).

Hepatitis A.typical_presentation: Jaundice, RUQ pain, fever, nausea after travel.
Hepatitis A.moa: Picornavirus causing acute hepatocyte swelling and necrosis.

Hypothyroidism.typical_presentation: Cold intolerance, weight gain, fatigue, dry skin, constipation.
Hypothyroidism.step1_buzzwords: Delayed reflex relaxation, Hurthle cells (Hashimoto's), Puffy face.
Hypothyroidism.lab_buzzwords: High TSH, Low T4, Anti-TPO antibodies.

Pheochromocytoma.typical_presentation: Episodic 5 P's: Pressure (HTN), Pain (Headache), Perspiration, Palpitations, Pallor.
Pheochromocytoma.moa: Tumor of chromaffin cells in adrenal medulla.
Pheochromocytoma.step1_buzzwords: Chromaffin cells, VMA, Phenoxybenzamine (alpha-block before beta).
Pheochromocytoma.treatment: Alpha-blocker followed by Beta-blocker, then surgical resection.

Esophageal Squamous Cell Carcinoma.risk_factors: Alcohol, Smoking, Achalasia, Caustic injury, Hot liquids.
Esophageal Squamous Cell Carcinoma.typical_presentation: Progressive dysphagia (solids then liquids), weight loss.
Esophageal Squamous Cell Carcinoma.moa: Malignant growth in upper/middle 1/3 of esophagus.
Esophageal Squamous Cell Carcinoma.lab_buzzwords: Biopsy: Squamous cells with keratin pearls.

Subarachnoid Hemorrhage (SAH).who_gets_it: Adults, often associated with Berry aneurysms.
Subarachnoid Hemorrhage (SAH).moa: Rupture of an aneurysm in the subarachnoid space.
Subarachnoid Hemorrhage (SAH).step1_buzzwords: Worst headache of life, Berry aneurysm, Xanthochromia.

Systemic Lupus Erythematosus (SLE).who_gets_it: Females of reproductive age; African American/Hispanic.
Systemic Lupus Erythematosus (SLE).typical_presentation: Malar rash, joint pain, photosensitivity, renal issues.
Systemic Lupus Erythematosus (SLE).moa: Type III hypersensitivity; autoantibodies against nuclear antigens.
Systemic Lupus Erythematosus (SLE).step1_buzzwords: Malar/Butterfly rash, Wire-loop lesions (kidney), Libman-Sacks endocarditis.

Stable Angina.who_gets_it: Older adults with CAD risk factors.
Stable Angina.typical_presentation: Chest pain on exertion relieved by rest or nitroglycerin.
Stable Angina.moa: Atherosclerosis causing >70% narrowing of coronary arteries.
Stable Angina.step1_buzzwords: Exertional pain, ST depression on EKG.

Prerenal Acute Kidney Injury (NSAID-Indu.typical_presentation: Decreased urine output, confusion, dry mucous membranes.
Prerenal Acute Kidney Injury (NSAID-Indu.moa: NSAIDs inhibit prostaglandins, causing afferent arteriole constriction.

Uncomplicated Cystitis (E. coli).moa: Ascending infection of the bladder by fecal flora.

Multiple Myeloma.typical_presentation: Bone pain, fractures, infections, fatigue, renal failure.
Multiple Myeloma.moa: Monoclonal plasma cell proliferation in the bone marrow.
Multiple Myeloma.step1_buzzwords: CRAB: Calcium high, Renal failure, Anemia, Bone lesions.

Myasthenia Gravis.typical_presentation: Ptosis, diplopia, muscle weakness that worsens with use.

Ischemic Stroke.who_gets_it: Elderly with HTN, AFib, or CAD.
Ischemic Stroke.moa: Embolic or thrombotic occlusion of a cerebral artery.
Ischemic Stroke.lab_buzzwords: Normal CT head (initially), DWI MRI positive.

Alzheimer Disease.typical_presentation: Slowly progressive memory loss, disorientation, paranoia.
Alzheimer Disease.moa: Beta-amyloid plaques and Tau neurofibrillary tangles.
Alzheimer Disease.step1_buzzwords: Amyloid plaques, Neurofibrillary tangles, Hippocampal atrophy.

Bacterial Meningitis.typical_presentation: Fever, headache, stiff neck, altered mental status.
Bacterial Meningitis.moa: Infection of the leptomeninges by bacteria.
Bacterial Meningitis.lab_buzzwords: CSF: Low Glucose, High Protein, High PMNs (neutrophils).

Congestive Heart Failure (CHF).risk_factors: HTN, Ischemic heart disease, Valvular disease.
Congestive Heart Failure (CHF).typical_presentation: Dyspnea, orthopnea, PND, bilateral leg edema.
Congestive Heart Failure (CHF).moa: Heart fails to pump blood efficiently; backward failure.
Congestive Heart Failure (CHF).step1_buzzwords: Paroxysmal Nocturnal Dyspnea, S3, Kerley B lines.

Urolithiasis (Kidney Stones).typical_presentation: Sudden flank pain radiating to groin (colicky).
Urolithiasis (Kidney Stones).moa: Supersaturation of urine leading to crystal formation.

Carcinoid Syndrome.typical_presentation: Diarrhea, Flushing, Wheezing, Right-heart valve lesions.
Carcinoid Syndrome.moa: Tumor secretes Serotonin after metastasizing to liver.

Testicular Torsion.typical_presentation: Acute severe testicular pain, high-riding testis, nausea.
Testicular Torsion.moa: Twisting of the spermatic cord cutting off blood supply.

Celiac Disease.who_gets_it: All ages; associated with Down Syndrome, Type 1 DM.
Celiac Disease.typical_presentation: Diarrhea, bloating, dermatitis herpetiformis, iron deficiency.
Celiac Disease.moa: Immune-mediated damage to small bowel villi due to gluten.

Pseudomonas Corneal Ulcer (Contact Lens-.typical_presentation: Eye pain, redness, blurry vision, hypopyon.
Pseudomonas Corneal Ulcer (Contact Lens-.moa: Bacterial infection of the corneal stroma.

Chronic Myeloid Leukemia (CML).typical_presentation: Fatigue, weight loss, massive splenomegaly, early satiety.
Chronic Myeloid Leukemia (CML).moa: t(9;22) BCR-ABL fusion creates a constitutive tyrosine kinase.
Chronic Myeloid Leukemia (CML).lab_buzzwords: Low LAP score (differentiates from leukemoid reaction).

Peripartum Cardiomyopathy.risk_factors: Last month of pregnancy or first 5 months postpartum.
Peripartum Cardiomyopathy.typical_presentation: Dyspnea, edema, orthopnea shortly after delivery.
Peripartum Cardiomyopathy.moa: Dilated cardiomyopathy of unknown etiology in late pregnancy.
Peripartum Cardiomyopathy.treatment: Heart failure meds (standard), avoid future pregnancy.

Urosepsis (Obstructive Pyelonephritis).who_gets_it: Elderly, patients with stones or BPH.
Urosepsis (Obstructive Pyelonephritis).moa: Infection above an obstruction entering the bloodstream.
Urosepsis (Obstructive Pyelonephritis).lab_buzzwords: WBC casts in urine (localizes to kidney).

Acute Pancreatitis.typical_presentation: Epigastric pain radiating to back, nausea, vomiting.
Acute Pancreatitis.moa: Autodigestion of pancreas by premature activation of trypsin.
Acute Pancreatitis.step1_buzzwords: Radiates to back, Cullen sign, Grey Turner sign.
Acute Pancreatitis.treatment: NPO, Aggressive IV fluids, Pain control.

Sarcoidosis.typical_presentation: Dry cough, dyspnea, erythema nodosum, uveitis.
Sarcoidosis.step1_buzzwords: Bilateral hilar lymphadenopathy, Erythema nodosum, ACE level.
Sarcoidosis.lab_buzzwords: High Calcium, High ACE, High Vitamin D.

Peritonsillar Abscess.typical_presentation: Severe sore throat, muffled 'hot potato' voice, trismus.
Peritonsillar Abscess.moa: Collection of pus between tonsillar capsule and pharyngeal constrictors.
Peritonsillar Abscess.step1_buzzwords: Hot potato voice, Uvular deviation, Trismus.

Rheumatoid Arthritis (RA).moa: Pannus formation and destruction of cartilage/bone.

Osteomyelitis.who_gets_it: IV drug users, diabetics, sickle cell patients.
Osteomyelitis.moa: Hematogenous or contiguous spread of infection to bone.
Osteomyelitis.step1_buzzwords: IV drug user, S. aureus, Sequestrum.

Anorexia Nervosa.typical_presentation: Low BMI, amenorrhea, lanugo, fear of weight gain.
Anorexia Nervosa.moa: Severe caloric restriction and distorted body image.

Normal Pressure Hydrocephalus.typical_presentation: Wet, Wacky, and Wobbly (Incontinence, Dementia, Ataxia).
Normal Pressure Hydrocephalus.moa: Impaired CSF absorption; ventriculomegaly without high pressure.
Normal Pressure Hydrocephalus.lab_buzzwords: Gait improvement after Large Volume Tap.

Dermatomyositis.typical_presentation: Proximal muscle weakness, eyelid rash, knuckle rash.
Dermatomyositis.step1_buzzwords: Heliotrope rash, Gottron papules, Shawl sign.

Myocardial Infarction (Heart Attack).typical_presentation: Crushing substernal chest pain, jaw/arm radiation, diaphoresis.
Myocardial Infarction (Heart Attack).moa: Coronary artery occlusion causing myocyte necrosis.
Myocardial Infarction (Heart Attack).step1_buzzwords: ST elevation, Coagulative necrosis, Jaw radiation.

Ulcerative Colitis (UC).moa: Continuous mucosal inflammation starting in the rectum.
Ulcerative Colitis (UC).step1_buzzwords: Lead pipe colon, Crypt abscesses, Rectal involvement.

Disseminated Gonococcal Infection (Septi.moa: Systemic spread of N. gonorrhoeae from mucosal site.

Parkinson Disease.typical_presentation: Resting tremor, bradykinesia, rigidity, postural instability.
Parkinson Disease.moa: Loss of dopaminergic neurons in the Substantia Nigra.

Gilbert Syndrome.lab_buzzwords: Normal LFTs, Isolated high indirect bilirubin.

Alcoholic Cardiomyopathy.typical_presentation: Heart failure (SOB, edema) in an alcoholic.
Alcoholic Cardiomyopathy.moa: Direct toxic effect of ethanol on myocardium (Dilated CM).

Retinal Detachment.typical_presentation: Gray curtain over vision, flashes (photopsia), floaters.
Retinal Detachment.moa: Separation of neurosensory retina from pigment epithelium.

Pulmonary Embolism (PE).risk_factors: Virchow Triad: Stasis, Hypercoagulability, Endothelial injury.
Pulmonary Embolism (PE).typical_presentation: Sudden dyspnea, pleuritic chest pain, tachycardia.
Pulmonary Embolism (PE).moa: Thrombus (usually from DVT) lodges in pulmonary artery.

Post-Streptococcal Glomerulonephritis.risk_factors: Recent Group A Strep infection (Pharyngitis or Impetigo).
Post-Streptococcal Glomerulonephritis.moa: Type III hypersensitivity; immune complex deposition.

Zenker Diverticulum.typical_presentation: Halitosis, regurgitation of undigested food, dysphagia.
Zenker Diverticulum.step1_buzzwords: Halitosis, Hot potato voice (if huge), Killian triangle.

C. Difficile Infection.typical_presentation: Profuse watery diarrhea, abdominal pain, fever.
C. Difficile Infection.moa: Toxin A (brush border) and Toxin B (cytoskeleton) damage.

Primary Biliary Cholangitis.moa: Autoimmune destruction of intrahepatic bile ducts.

Cauda Equina Syndrome.who_gets_it: Adults with disc disease or tumors.
Cauda Equina Syndrome.typical_presentation: Saddle anesthesia, bowel/bladder dysfunction, leg weakness.
Cauda Equina Syndrome.moa: Compression of multiple lumbosacral nerve roots.

Pelvic Inflammatory Disease (Chlamydia-A.risk_factors: Multiple partners, lack of barrier protection.
Pelvic Inflammatory Disease (Chlamydia-A.typical_presentation: Cervical motion tenderness, adnexal pain, discharge.
Pelvic Inflammatory Disease (Chlamydia-A.moa: Ascending infection of the upper genital tract.

Aortic Stenosis.moa: Narrowing of the aortic valve orifice.

Adult-Onset Still Disease.typical_presentation: Daily spiking fevers, evanescent salmon rash, arthritis.

Gout.moa: Precipitation of monosodium urate crystals in joints.

Anti-MDA5 Dermatomyositis.who_gets_it: Adults; specifically seen in East Asian populations.
Anti-MDA5 Dermatomyositis.typical_presentation: Knuckle rash, skin ulcers, rapidly progressive lung disease.
Anti-MDA5 Dermatomyositis.moa: Autoimmune attack on skin and lung vasculature.

Aortic Dissection (Marfan Syndrome).typical_presentation: Tearing chest pain, lens dislocation, hypermobility.
Aortic Dissection (Marfan Syndrome).moa: Cystic medial necrosis of the aorta.

Malignant Pleural Mesothelioma.typical_presentation: Dyspnea, chest pain, weight loss, pleural effusions.

Anti-NMDA Receptor Encephalitis.moa: Autoantibodies against the GluN1 subunit of NMDA receptor.

Disseminated Histoplasmosis.who_gets_it: Residents of Ohio/Mississippi river valleys; HIV patients.
Disseminated Histoplasmosis.step1_buzzwords: Histo hides in macrophages, Ohio River Valley.

Irritable Bowel Syndrome (IBS).typical_presentation: Abdominal pain relieved by defecation, bloating.
Irritable Bowel Syndrome (IBS).moa: Altered gut motility and visceral hypersensitivity.

Amyotrophic Lateral Sclerosis (ALS).typical_presentation: Mixed UMN and LMN signs, fasciculations, normal sensation.
Amyotrophic Lateral Sclerosis (ALS).moa: Degeneration of upper and lower motor neurons.
